metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the container/host boundary for hook execution: specify either (a) thin Bash wrapper scripts (checked into the repo, bind-mounted into `$site/hooks/`) that invoke g2p on the HOST via a side-channel (e.g., a host-side daemon, a named pipe, or `nsenter`), or (b) a host-side Gerrit hooks executor (e.g., running Gerrit in host-network mode with hooks at a shared path), or (c) confirm that the hooks volume will be bind-mounted to the HOST filesystem where the Python interpreter is also present — and update the docker-compose.yml volumes and mechanism description to match.
findings.finding: The plan's core hook-execution mechanism is broken: Gerrit's `hooks` plugin execs hook scripts from `$site/hooks/` as seen from INSIDE the container. The plan installs g2p on the HOST (Python ≥3.11, not present in the container) and symlinks its console-script hooks into the bind-mounted `$site/hooks/`. When a `patchset-created` event fires, Gerrit calls `exec($site/hooks/patchset-created)` from within the container — but the symlink target (a host Python console-script path like `/usr/local/bin/patchset-created`) does not exist inside the Java-only container. The stated rationale ('Java-only image, no Python') correctly explains why g2p can't run inside the container, but this makes the symlink-into-$site/hooks approach WORSE: the hooks would be exec'd by a container process that cannot resolve the host Python path. Production g2p deployments on bare-metal/VM hosts work because the hook scripts and their Python interpreter share the same filesystem — here they don't.
findings.location: What / AC2 — 'symlink the patchset-created / comment-added / change-merged console-script hooks into the bind-mounted $site/hooks/'
findings.suggested_fix: The standard production pattern for this topology is: (1) add `gerrit_hooks` as a bind volume in docker-compose.yml pointing to a host path (e.g., `/var/gerrit/site/hooks`), (2) write thin Bash hook wrappers at that path that are executable within the container AND call a host-side script — but since the container can only exec its own filesystem, the wrapper must itself be a self-contained Bash script (not a symlink to a host Python binary). The Bash wrapper can use `curl` to POST to a host-side g2p HTTP receiver, or the hooks can be pure-Bash scripts that call `curl` the GitHub Dispatch API directly. Alternatively, if the Gerrit site hooks dir is a true bind-mount shared with the host filesystem AND the container has `#!/usr/bin/env python3` available (it doesn't), the current approach would work — but it doesn't because Python isn't in the container.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `gerrit_hooks` bind-mount (e.g., `/var/gerrit/site/hooks:/var/gerrit/hooks`) to `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` and include `docker-compose.yml` in the file_impact list, since the hooks directory must be visible inside the container for the hooks plugin to execute the g2p symlinks.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that symlinks will be placed into the 'bind-mounted `$site/hooks/`', but `docker-compose.yml` contains no bind-mount or named volume for a `hooks/` subdirectory — only git, index, cache, db, etc, logs, and plugins are mounted.
findings.location: AC2: 'symlink the `patchset-created` / `comment-added` / `change-merged` console-script hooks into the bind-mounted `$site/hooks/`'
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to docker-compose.yml volumes under gerrit service:
  - /var/gerrit/site/hooks:/var/gerrit/hooks
And declare external: true for a gerrit_hooks volume (or use a direct host bind) in the volumes section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` to the edit-set and include a `gerrit_hooks` external volume (or direct bind-mount of `/var/gerrit/site/hooks` → `/var/gerrit/hooks`) so g2p hook symlinks are visible inside the Gerrit container.
findings.finding: `docker-compose.yml` is not listed as an edit target, yet the plan's AC requires symlinking g2p hooks into the bind-mounted `$site/hooks/` — a directory that has no corresponding volume or bind-mount entry in the current `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` (only `git, index, cache, db, etc, logs, plugins` are mounted). Without adding a `gerrit_hooks` external volume (or direct bind mount for `/var/gerrit/site/hooks`), the symlinks would not be visible inside the Gerrit container.
findings.location: AC: 'symlink … hooks into the bind-mounted $site/hooks/' / infra/compose/docker-compose.yml (volumes section)
findings.suggested_fix: In `docker-compose.yml` under the `gerrit` service `volumes:` block add `- gerrit_hooks:/var/gerrit/hooks`, declare `gerrit_hooks: external: true` in the top-level `volumes:` section, and extend `compose-up.sh`'s loop to create the `hooks` subdir and external volume alongside the existing seven.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `gerrit_hooks` bind-mount (or direct host bind-mount on `/var/gerrit/site/hooks`) to `docker-compose.yml` so that host-side g2p symlinks in `$site/hooks/` are visible to the container's `hooks` plugin at `/var/gerrit/hooks/`.
findings.finding: The `$site/hooks/` directory is not bind-mounted into the Gerrit container — docker-compose.yml defines only 7 named volumes (git, index, cache, db, etc, logs, plugins) with no `gerrit_hooks` entry — so host-side symlinks placed there will not be visible to the `hooks` plugin running inside the container.
findings.location: AC2 / infra/compose/docker-compose.yml (volumes section)
findings.suggested_fix: In docker-compose.yml, add a new external named volume `gerrit_hooks` (mirroring the pattern of gerrit_plugins) bound to `${SITE_HOST_DIR}/hooks`, and mount it as `- gerrit_hooks:/var/gerrit/hooks` in the gerrit service; then ensure compose-up.sh creates that bind volume. Alternatively use a direct bind mount: `- /var/gerrit/site/hooks:/var/gerrit/hooks`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (via `docker run gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 java -jar gerrit.war init --list-plugins` or equivalent) that `hooks` is bundled in gerrit.war for 3.14.1, and update `install-plugins.sh` + `plugins/README.md` with the confirmed enablement command.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `hooks` is a 'core plugin bundled in gerrit.war' enableable via `init --install-plugin` / copy with no download — but no file in the codebase (install-plugins.sh, plugins/README.md, gerrit.config, docker-compose.yml, setup scripts) mentions the `hooks` plugin at all, leaving the assertion unverifiable from the repo.
findings.location: What section / AC1: 'Enable the `hooks` + `replication` core plugins (from `gerrit.war` via `init --install-plugin` / copy — no download or sha256 pin)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a host Python ≥3.11 provisioning step (e.g., `dnf install -y python3.11 python3.11-pip`) to the operator runbook or a new install script, and verify the assumption in `plugins/README.md` or the ADR.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Install g2p on the host (Python ≥3.11)' as a prerequisite, but no host-side script in the repo provisions Python 3.11+ on the AL2023 box — `install-certbot-timer.sh` installs a generic `python3` via dnf without a version constraint, and no other provisioning script establishes or checks for Python ≥3.11.
findings.location: AC2: 'Install g2p on the host (Python ≥3.11)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the install script or runbook: `dnf install -y python3.11 python3.11-pip && python3.11 -m pip install gerrit-to-platform` and gate the rest of the AC2 steps on `python3.11 --version`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and document in `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md` (and a runbook step) whether the `hooks` plugin in `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` is already auto-enabled (like `webhooks`) or requires explicit `init --install-plugin hooks` / copy-from-war, and specify the exact command in AC1.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the Gerrit `hooks` core plugin is 'bundled in `gerrit.war`' and can be enabled via `init --install-plugin` / copy 'with no download or sha256 pin' — but no codebase artifact verifies whether `hooks` is already auto-enabled in the `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` image (as `webhooks` is, per `plugins/README.md`) or requires an explicit enablement step. The existing `plugins/README.md` does not mention `hooks` at all, and `install-plugins.sh` has no logic for core-plugin enablement via init or copy-from-war. The 'verified' claim in the parent epic is in-ticket only, not reflected in any committed code.
findings.location: AC1 — 'Enable the `hooks` + `replication` core plugins (from `gerrit.war` via `init --install-plugin` / copy — no download or sha256 pin)'
findings.suggested_fix: Inspect the running or pulled image: `docker run --rm --entrypoint sh gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 -c 'ls /var/gerrit/plugins/'` to see if `hooks.jar` is present, and `docker run --rm --entrypoint sh gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 -c 'cat /var/gerrit/etc/gerrit.config'` to see if it is listed under `[plugins] installPlugin`. Record the result in `plugins/README.md` under a new `hooks` row alongside the existing `webhooks` row, and update AC1 to name the exact command (or confirm 'already enabled, no action needed').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact mechanism that keeps the PAT out of `gerrit_to_platform.ini` on disk (e.g., g2p env-var token reference + boot-time injection by an extended `fetch-secrets.sh`, or a 0600 key-file path that g2p reads instead of an inline token), and add the corresponding SSM parameter slot to `ssm.tf` and `fetch-secrets.sh`.
findings.finding: The plan's AC3 asserts 'GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4 — no plaintext on disk,' but `gerrit_to_platform.ini` is a static INI file that g2p reads at hook-invocation time and the plan specifies no mechanism to keep the PAT out of it (e.g., env-var substitution, boot-time template expansion, or a key-file indirection). The existing `fetch-secrets.sh` writes only to `infra/compose/.env` for containers; `ssm.tf` has no g2p PAT slot. An implementer who writes the PAT literal into the INI file contradicts the plan's own 'no plaintext on disk' claim while technically following every other AC.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'Write gerrit_to_platform.ini (GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4 — no plaintext on disk)'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend `fetch-secrets.sh` to also write a 0600 host-side secrets file (e.g., `/etc/gerrit-to-platform/secrets.env`) containing `G2P_GITHUB_PAT=<value>`, add `/rebar/prod/g2p-github-pat` to `ssm.tf`'s `rebar_secret_params`, configure `gerrit_to_platform.ini` to reference `token = ${G2P_GITHUB_PAT}` (or whatever env-var key g2p supports), and source the secrets file in the hook wrapper script before invoking g2p.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that posts a `recheck` comment on the test patchset (or a dedicated test change) and asserts that a second `workflow_dispatch` event fires (verifying the `comment-added` → `recheck → verify` mapping end-to-end).
findings.finding: The `recheck → verify` comment-added flow is a new user-facing CI-retrigger path but has no acceptance criterion testing it — not even a happy-path check. AC #4 only exercises the `patchset-created` path (push a test patchset); no AC asks the reviewer to post a `recheck` comment and verify that `comment-added` fires a second `workflow_dispatch`.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #3 (`recheck → verify` mapping) and AC #4 (coexistence proof)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC #4 or add AC #5: 'After the patchset-created dispatch is observed, post a comment containing `recheck` on the same change and verify a second `workflow_dispatch` is dispatched to the CI workflow within N seconds, confirming the comment-added hook and recheck→verify mapping are correctly wired.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the required `gerrit_to_platform.ini` key-value schema (at minimum: GitHub org/repo, PAT env-var name, dispatcher URL, and hook-to-workflow mapping) so the file can be written without stopping to look up g2p documentation.
findings.finding: The `gerrit_to_platform.ini` contents are not specified: the plan names the file but omits the required INI keys (GitHub org, repo, dispatcher endpoint, PAT env-var reference, event-to-label mapping, etc.), leaving an executor to guess the schema.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3 / Scope bullet 'gerrit_to_platform.ini'
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a commented skeleton INI to the plan, e.g.:
```ini
[gerrit]
url = https://gerrit.example.com

[github]
org = <org>
repo = <repo>
token_env = GH_PAT   # populated by S4 SSM materialization

[hooks]
patchset-created = workflow_dispatch
comment-added = workflow_dispatch   # filtered by recheck pattern
change-merged = workflow_dispatch
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify where the `recheck → verify` mapping lives (INI key, g2p config section, or separate file) and its exact syntax/regex so it can be implemented and tested deterministically.
findings.finding: The `recheck → verify` comment mapping is mentioned but neither its location (INI section? separate file?) nor its exact syntax is specified, leaving the executor without enough information to implement it deterministically.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3 / Scope bullet 'the recheck → verify comment mapping'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the full symlink source and target paths for `replication.config` (e.g., `ln -s /opt/gerrit-config/replication.config $GERRIT_SITE/etc/replication.config`).
findings.finding: The `replication.config` symlink's source and target are not specified: the plan says 'replication.config symlink' but does not state what file on the host is being symlinked, from which directory, to which directory inside the Gerrit site.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3 / Scope bullet 'replication.config symlink'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify what 'replication.config symlink' means in AC3 — if it refers to a g2p-specific config snippet, name the source file and explain how it coexists with `setup-replication.sh`'s existing copy of `infra/gerrit/replication.config` to `/var/gerrit/site/etc/replication.config`.
findings.finding: The plan calls for a '`replication.config` symlink' (in Scope and AC3), but the existing `infra/gerrit/setup-replication.sh` performs a file COPY (`cp`) to `$GERRIT_SITE/etc/replication.config`, not a symlink — these two approaches are mutually exclusive and the plan's assertion of a 'symlink' contradicts the in-place mechanism.
findings.location: Scope: '`replication.config` symlink'; AC3: 'the `replication.config` symlink'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC line 3: if 'replication.config symlink' refers to a g2p-specific config file (not the `replication` plugin's `etc/replication.config`), rename it to avoid confusion with the S5 artifact; if it means keeping the existing copy-based install, remove the word 'symlink'.
findings.finding: The plan refers to a '`replication.config` symlink' in AC line 3, but the existing `setup-replication.sh` (the S5 artifact for this file) **copies** `infra/gerrit/replication.config` into the site `etc/` directory — it does not use a symlink. The plan's use of 'symlink' for `replication.config` is ambiguous: it is either an inaccurate description of the existing copy mechanism, or it implies a new installation method that would conflict with `setup-replication.sh`.
findings.location: AC line 3: 'the `replication.config` symlink' / infra/gerrit/setup-replication.sh

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact mechanism by which the GitHub PAT reaches g2p at hook-invocation time: name whether it flows via (a) a host-side materialization script writing a 0600 `gerrit_to_platform.ini` (analogous to `materialize-deploy-key.sh`), (b) a `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var set in a g2p invocation wrapper, or (c) another mechanism — and confirm the chosen path is consistent with the existing SSM SecureString materialization pattern.
findings.finding: The plan states the GitHub PAT will be 'sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4 — no plaintext on disk', yet `gerrit_to_platform.ini` is the file g2p reads its token from at hook-invocation time. The existing SSM materialization pattern (e.g., `materialize-deploy-key.sh`) writes secrets to 0600 files on the host FS — that IS 'on disk'. The plan does not specify whether: (a) g2p is configured to read the token from an env var injected at invocation (supported by g2p via `GITHUB_TOKEN` env), (b) the ini file is itself the materialized 0600 file written by a new host-side materialization script (which IS on disk, but correctly scoped), or (c) some other mechanism. Without specifying the PAT-to-g2p handoff, the 'no plaintext on disk' claim is unverifiable and the implementation cannot be written unambiguously.
findings.location: AC3 — 'Write gerrit_to_platform.ini (GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4 — no plaintext on disk)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify what `replication.config` symlink in AC3 actually means — if g2p needs a `replication.config` reference, specify what it points to and confirm it does not conflict with the S2-owned `$site/etc/replication.config` managed by `setup-replication.sh`.
findings.finding: The plan's AC3 describes creating a `replication.config` symlink, but S2 (a declared hard dependency) already writes a real file at `$site/etc/replication.config` via `setup-replication.sh` (which uses `cp`, not `ln -s`). Creating a symlink at that path would silently overwrite the S2-managed config, breaking the S5 Gerrit→GitHub mirror.
findings.location: AC3 — 'the `replication.config` symlink' / infra/gerrit/setup-replication.sh line 90

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the 'replication.config symlink' in AC3/Scope replaces or supplements the existing `setup-replication.sh` copy pattern, and if a symlink genuinely offers value over the established `cp + chown` operator-deployment model documented in ADR-0010; if not, remove it and use the copy pattern consistently.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a 'replication.config symlink' (AC3/Scope) where the established pattern in `setup-replication.sh` is a controlled operator-run `cp + chown` into `$site/etc/replication.config`, as documented in ADR-0010. A symlink creates a second config-surface loading path with different semantics (live edits to the repo copy propagate immediately, bypassing the operator deployment step) and no justification for the added complexity.
findings.location: AC3 / Scope: 'replication.config symlink'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the 'replication.config symlink' from AC3/Scope and rely on the existing `setup-replication.sh` copy mechanism (already tested and ADR-documented). If S3 needs the replication plugin enabled, the existing copy approach already handles it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add `/rebar/prod/github-actions-pat` (or similar) to `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`'s `rebar_secret_params` list as part of this story's edit-set, or explicitly call out in the plan which story (S3 vs S4) provisions that SSM parameter slot and update the scope section accordingly.
findings.finding: `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` is not named as an edit target, yet the plan states the GitHub PAT for `gerrit_to_platform.ini` will be 'sourced via host-side SSM materialization from S4'. The current `ssm.tf` defines no `/rebar/prod/github-pat` (or equivalent) parameter — no such slot exists in the `rebar_secret_params` list. If S4 does not provision this SSM parameter, S3 cannot satisfy the 'no plaintext on disk' requirement.
findings.location: AC: 'GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4 — no plaintext on disk' / infra/terraform/ssm.tf

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the GitHub Actions PAT SSM parameter slot (e.g. `/rebar/prod/github-actions-pat`, type `SecureString`, value `CHANGEME`, `ignore_changes = [value]`) to `local.rebar_secret_params` in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` so the slot's existence is Terraform-managed, consistent with all other secrets.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new GitHub PAT credential consumed by `gerrit_to_platform.ini` but does not add a corresponding SSM parameter placeholder entry to `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` (the Terraform-managed secret-slot registry). The existing `rebar_secret_params` list in ssm.tf has no entry for a GitHub Actions PAT (e.g. `/rebar/prod/github-actions-pat`). Without a Terraform-managed placeholder, the secret slot won't exist until S4 manually creates it out-of-band, creating an ordering dependency that is invisible in the IaC and breaks the 'Terraform owns the parameter's existence + type, not its value' contract already established for all other secrets.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4'); infra/terraform/ssm.tf (local.rebar_secret_params)
findings.suggested_fix: In `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`, add `"/rebar/prod/github-actions-pat"` to the `rebar_secret_params` local list. The existing `for_each` loop will create the SecureString placeholder; S4 then populates the real value out-of-band, matching the established pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the atomic write protocol for `gerrit_to_platform.ini`: specify that S4's materialization writes to a temp file and renames atomically, document who owns the file's CREATE (S3 scaffold with placeholder, or S4 first-write), and enumerate how g2p hooks tolerate an absent/stale ini at startup.
findings.finding: The `gerrit_to_platform.ini` file has split ownership across S3 (defines structure) and S4 (provides the PAT secret), but the plan does not define how the file is atomically created or updated when the PAT is rotated — a PAT rotation could leave a partially-written ini file that racing hook invocations read mid-write.
findings.location: AC line 3 — 'Write gerrit_to_platform.ini (GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4 — no plaintext on disk)'
findings.suggested_fix: Use write-to-tempfile + `os.rename()` (atomic on Linux same-filesystem) in S4's materialization script; S3 should scaffold a stub ini so hooks fail-fast with a clear error rather than a missing-file traceback.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a RETIRE path to the plan: document how hook symlinks and `gerrit_to_platform.ini` are removed (e.g., an Ansible task or cleanup script), and ensure the install procedure is idempotent (re-running it doesn't create duplicate symlinks).
findings.finding: The plan has no RETIRE/cleanup path for the hook symlinks, `gerrit_to_platform.ini`, or the g2p Python install — if g2p is decommissioned, stale symlinks in `$site/hooks/` will cause Gerrit to execute missing executables on every hook event.
findings.location: Scope section / What section — no decommission or cleanup path mentioned

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete in-session proving command for each of AC1 (e.g., `docker exec gerrit java -jar /var/gerrit/bin/gerrit.war ls-plugins`), AC2 (e.g., `ls -la $GERRIT_SITE/hooks/patchset-created`), and AC3 (e.g., assert INI contains `%(GH_PAT)s` or equivalent env-var reference, not a literal token).
findings.finding: ACs 1, 2, and 3 have no stated proving commands: plugin enablement, hook symlinks, and config file correctness each need a concrete in-session check, but none is provided.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1 / AC2 / AC3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the exact plugin-installation method (e.g., `java -jar gerrit.war init --install-plugin hooks --install-plugin replication -d $site` vs. copying JARs into `$site/plugins/`) so the executor has a single, deterministic path.
findings.finding: The plugin installation method is given as two options separated by a slash ('init --install-plugin / copy'), which are operationally different; an executor cannot choose between them without additional guidance.
findings.location: What section: 'Enable core plugins; update infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md' / AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'observe' in AC4 with a concrete proving command and required artifact, e.g.: `gh run list --workflow=<workflow-file> --limit=1` shows a triggered run AND Gerrit's `$site/logs/hooks.log` shows a zero-exit `workflow_dispatch` call for the same Change-Id.
findings.finding: AC4 (coexistence proof) uses 'observe' as the verification verb with no concrete proving command or artifact, making it non-deterministically verifiable; an executor cannot confirm done-ness without guessing what to inspect.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit dependency on the review-bot system (or the story that delivers it) to the Scope section, and briefly describe it in the plan body so that AC#4's 'LLM-Review vote' coexistence check is traceable to a concrete, known-operational component.
findings.finding: AC#4 references 'review-bot webhook' and 'LLM-Review vote' as part of the coexistence proof, but neither the review-bot nor its LLM-Review vote label is described anywhere in Why/What/Scope, and no dependency on the review-bot system is declared alongside S2/S4.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC#4
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add 'Depends on: review-bot being operational (delivers the LLM-Review label)' alongside S2/S4. In the What section, add one line noting that the coexistence test assumes the review-bot webhook is already deployed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document whether the `replication.config` symlink in AC#3 replaces the S2-written file (and if so, what the symlink target is), and add an explicit back-out step (e.g., restore the original file from backup or re-run S2's provisioning) in case the symlink breaks replication.
findings.finding: The `replication.config` symlink step in AC#3 could overwrite the `replication.config` written by the S2 story (which is a declared dependency), yet the plan provides no rollback path or back-out procedure if the symlink operation corrupts or replaces the S2-managed config.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC#3; What section ('replication.config symlink')
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, clarify: 'The replication.config symlink replaces [or supplements] the file written by S2; the original is backed up to replication.config.bak before symlinking, allowing rollback via `mv replication.config.bak replication.config`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify g2p hook failure handling: configure retry/backoff for `workflow_dispatch` calls (or confirm g2p's built-in retry is sufficient), define a timeout, and add an observability path (log aggregation + alert) so operators can detect and debug silent hook failures.
findings.finding: The plan introduces an external HTTP call (g2p `workflow_dispatch` to GitHub Actions) with no specified retry, backoff, timeout, or graceful-degradation behavior — if GitHub is unavailable or returns a transient 5xx, the CI hook silently fails and operators have no signal.
findings.location: What / AC line 2 — 'symlink its hooks into the bind-mounted $site/hooks/' and implicit g2p operation
findings.suggested_fix: Document in the plan: (1) g2p's retry config knob (e.g., `max_retries`, `retry_backoff` in `gerrit_to_platform.ini`); (2) hook stdout/stderr routed to a named log file or syslog; (3) a monitoring alert on non-zero hook exit codes or absence of `workflow_dispatch` events over a threshold window.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability AC: specify where g2p hook stdout/stderr is captured (e.g., syslog, a named log file under `$site/logs/`), define at least one alert condition (hook non-zero exit or absence of dispatch events), and document how SSM materialization failures are surfaced.
findings.finding: The plan has no observability instrumentation for the new failure points: hook execution errors, SSM materialization failures, and failed `workflow_dispatch` calls are not wired to any metric, log destination, or alert — leaving operators blind to failures in production.
findings.location: What section / Acceptance Criteria — no AC or implementation note covers monitoring/logging/alerting

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the review-bot webhook (or its owning epic/story) as an explicit dependency in the Scope section, or provide an alternative AC4 validation path that does not require review-bot to be live (e.g., verify workflow_dispatch independently, then note coexistence proof is contingent on review-bot availability).
findings.finding: AC4's coexistence proof depends on the review-bot webhook ('LLM-Review' vote) being operational at test time, but 'review-bot' is not listed as a dependency in the Scope/Dependencies section. If the review-bot is not running or not yet integrated in the target environment, the end-state-observable proof for this ticket cannot be demonstrated, leaving AC4 unverifiable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 (Coexistence proof); Scope section (dependencies list)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add: 'Depends on: review-bot webhook operational (LLM-Review vote) for AC4 coexistence proof.' Alternatively, split AC4 into two sub-checks: (a) verify g2p fires workflow_dispatch independently; (b) once review-bot is live, confirm both votes appear on the same event.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/compose/gerrit.config` to the edit-set with an explicit `hooks` plugin enablement directive (e.g. a `[gerrit] installPlugin = hooks` or the site-init equivalent) so the plugin is loaded on every Gerrit boot.
findings.finding: `infra/compose/gerrit.config` is not listed as an edit target, yet enabling the `hooks` core plugin requires adding a `[gerrit] installPlugin = hooks` stanza (or equivalent `[plugins]` configuration) to `gerrit.config`. The current file has no hooks-plugin enablement entry; the Gerrit `hooks` plugin is bundled in `gerrit.war` but must be explicitly enabled.
findings.location: AC: 'Enable the `hooks` + `replication` core plugins' / infra/compose/gerrit.config

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add observability for g2p hook dispatch failures: log hook exit codes/stderr to journald and extend `infra/scripts/observability.sh` (or add a new probe section) to count g2p errors and publish a `rebar/host:g2p_dispatch_errors` CloudWatch metric, with a corresponding alarm in `infra/terraform/` consistent with the existing monitoring pattern.
findings.finding: The plan installs a new outbound hook daemon (g2p) that makes GitHub API calls on every `patchset-created` / `comment-added` / `change-merged` event, but specifies no observability for g2p hook dispatch failures (e.g. GitHub API errors, expired PAT, rate-limit responses). The existing observability probe (`infra/scripts/observability.sh`) covers replication errors, voter errors, gate reachability, and mirror sync — but nothing for g2p `workflow_dispatch` failures. A broken PAT or GitHub API outage would silently drop CI triggers with no alert.
findings.location: What / Scope sections; Acceptance Criteria AC2–AC3; infra/scripts/observability.sh

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `gerrit init --install-plugin hooks` works against the live `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` container (or confirm the copy-based fallback — `docker run --entrypoint sh … cp hooks.jar /seed/` — is the actual delivery path), and replace the ambiguous 'init --install-plugin / copy' with a single, tested mechanism.
findings.finding: The `gerrit init --install-plugin` subcommand, named as the mechanism to enable the `hooks` core plugin, is UNVERIFIED in this containerized setup — no usage of `gerrit init` appears anywhere in the codebase, and the container image uses a fixed entrypoint that boots Gerrit directly rather than running `init`.
findings.location: AC1 — 'from `gerrit.war` via `init --install-plugin` / copy'
findings.suggested_fix: Follow the existing compose-up.sh pattern: use `docker run --rm --entrypoint sh gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 -c 'cp /var/gerrit/plugins/hooks.jar /seed/'` (after confirming `hooks.jar` exists in the image's baked plugins dir) rather than invoking `gerrit init`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC3 (and the ADR) that the PAT MUST be a **fine-grained, single-repo-scoped** token targeting only `navapbc/rebar` with the minimum permissions (`Actions: write`, `Contents: read`, `Metadata: read`), explicitly ruling out a classic org-wide PAT.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether the GitHub PAT used by g2p is a fine-grained, single-repo-scoped PAT or a classic (org-wide) PAT. 'Actions r/w, Contents+Metadata r' are classic PAT scopes that, if granted as a classic token, apply to every repository in the GitHub organisation — significantly over-privileged relative to the minimum needed (trigger `workflow_dispatch` on one repo, `navapbc/rebar`).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'Write gerrit_to_platform.ini (GitHub PAT sourced via the host-side SSM materialization from S4 — no plaintext on disk)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'The PAT MUST be a GitHub fine-grained PAT scoped to the single repository `navapbc/rebar` with permissions: Actions (write), Contents (read), Metadata (read) — a classic PAT or an org-scoped token is NOT acceptable.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
